marketOverview: Markets enter the week in a stagflation framework that intensified through the prior week. Trump's primetime address confirmed no ceasefire is imminent — the US will 'hit Iran very hard over the next 2-3 weeks,' with threats to strike Iranian electric plants and oil facilities. Brent settled above $112/bbl, WTI above $103/bbl. Central banks across G10 have violently repriced from cuts to hikes. Monday is a near-universal bank holiday (AUD, NZD, CHF, EUR, GBP all closed), meaning the week effectively starts Tuesday. The dominant themes are: (1) USD stagflation premium — CPI on Friday is the critical catalyst; (2) JPY normalization — BOJ hawkish, verbal intervention defended 160, Tankan beat; (3) Antipodean weakness — AUD and NZD remain the cleanest shorts in a risk-off, high-beta environment; (4) RBNZ rate decision on Wednesday — hold expected at 2.25% but statement tone matters; (5) USD Core PCE + Final GDP on Thursday and CPI on Friday are the two biggest USD events of the week.

currencies.name: US Dollar
currencies.rationale: USD finished the prior week at the top of the G10 board on a stagflation framework: surging oil feeds inflation, forces Fed hike repricing, and drives haven demand simultaneously. ISM Services beat (54.0 vs 54.8 exp — slight miss but still expansionary), while the NFP print from Good Friday is already in the market. The week ahead is dominated by Core PCE + Final GDP on Thursday and the critical CPI print on Friday — a hot CPI would cement the bullish case and push rate-hike expectations further. Fed speakers (Goolsbee, Jefferson, Daly) are all on the calendar and are expected to maintain the hawkish tone. USD is the cleanest expression of the stagflation trade.
drivers: Stagflation framework: oil above $100/bbl feeds inflation, forces Fed hike repricing
ISM Services PMI 54.0 (Mon) — expansionary; services sector holding up
Core PCE + Final GDP (Thu 13:30 FFT) — key inflation and growth check
CPI m/m + CPI y/y (Fri 13:30 FFT) — CRITICAL; hot print cements bullish case
Fed speakers (Goolsbee Tue, Jefferson Tue, Daly Wed) expected hawkish

events.event: Core PCE + Final GDP + Unemployment Claims (13:30 FFT)

currencies.name: Euro
currencies.rationale: EUR had a messy week — it held up briefly on softer aggregate CPI (2.5% YoY vs 2.8% exp, Core 2.3% vs 2.4% exp) and a temporary oil pullback, but by week-end the market returned to treating the Eurozone as exposed in an energy shock. The ECB hawkish repricing is still intact (two hikes priced by year-end), and EUR finished as the third strongest currency on the week — but the macro base remains fragile. Near-term neutral: the rates story held up better than expected and kept EUR supported into the weekly close, but structural vulnerabilities (energy importer, no fiscal union, BTP-Bund spread fragmentation risk) cap the upside. Monday is a bank holiday across France, Germany, and Italy.
drivers: ECB hawkish repricing intact: two 25bp hikes priced by year-end
CPI Flash miss (2.5% vs 2.8% exp) — energy shock not yet fully filtering through
EUR finished third strongest on the week despite difficult macro backdrop
BTP-Bund spread fragmentation risk caps medium-term upside
Final Services PMI (Tue 10:00 FFT) and Retail Sales (Wed 10:00 FFT) are key events

currencies.name: British Pound
currencies.rationale: Sterling had a strong midweek spell but could not hold up once the broader market swung back toward the Dollar and a harder risk-off tone. The BOE repricing is still supporting the Pound at the front end (~55bps of tightening priced, BofA forecasting two hikes in June and July), but it is doing so inside a fragile macro backdrop where higher yields are not automatically positive. BOE Governor Bailey warned markets are 'getting ahead of themselves.' Near-term neutral: the rates story can keep GBP supported if inflation worries dominate, but Sterling's risk-sensitive character means it struggles when sentiment worsens materially. Monday is a bank holiday. Final Services PMI on Tuesday is the key event.
drivers: BOE repricing: ~55bps of tightening priced; BofA forecasts two hikes (June + July)
BOE Bailey: 'markets getting ahead of themselves' — limits clean bullish case
FTSE 100 outperformed on oil majors (BP, Shell) and miners
US-UK pharmaceutical pricing agreement concluded — trade positive
Final Services PMI (Tue 10:30 FFT) is the key event

currencies.name: Japanese Yen
currencies.rationale: JPY had a better week than the broader tone would normally allow. The reversal from above 160 showed the market still respects both intervention risk and the domestic normalization story. BOJ's March Summary of Opinions was distinctly hawkish — warning against falling 'behind the curve,' citing tightening labor markets, strong Shunto wages (5%+), and the inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict. BOJ announced JGB purchase trims across multiple maturities for Q2. Tankan Large Manufacturing beat expectations, cementing near-term BOJ hike expectations. Near-term weak bullish: haven + creditor nation + BOJ normalization all active, but wide US-Japan yield differentials remain the key constraint.
drivers: BOJ hawkish: March Summary of Opinions warns against falling 'behind the curve'
Shunto wage growth 5%+ — BOJ normalization story intact; Q2 JGB purchase trims announced
Tankan Large Manufacturing beat — business sentiment confirms near-term BOJ hike
USD/JPY defended 160 on verbal intervention (Mimura, Ueda, Katayama)
Consumer Confidence (Thu 06:00 FFT) and PPI y/y (Thu 00:50 FFT) are key events

currencies.name: Australian Dollar
currencies.rationale: AUD was one of the primary casualties of the deteriorating global risk environment — heavily sold as the US-Iran conflict escalated and equity markets stumbled under surging energy prices. Despite the RBA being repriced toward tighter policy (~62bps of tightening by year-end), the global energy shock and risk-off environment overwhelm the domestic support story. AUD is a high-beta, risk-sensitive currency tightly linked to China and commodities — when those drivers become unstable, the Aussie struggles. Monday is a bank holiday. No major AUD data events this week — the currency will trade as a pure risk sentiment and China proxy.
drivers: High-beta risk proxy — primary casualty of US-Iran escalation and equity weakness
RBA repriced to ~62bps of tightening by year-end — imported inflation, not domestic strength
LNG facility outage (Cyclone Narelle) compounded global energy supply risks
Japan PM Takaichi planning Australia visit — rare-earth supply chains and Hormuz cooperation
No major AUD data events — pure risk sentiment and China proxy

currencies.name: Canadian Dollar
currencies.rationale: CAD handled the prior week better than other cyclical commodity currencies — WTI briefly above $113/bbl provided underlying support even as risk appetite soured. Canada's January GDP beat (0.1% vs flat exp), February GDP estimated at 0.2%, and exports rose 6.4%. The BoC has been repriced to ~44bps of tightening by year-end. Near-term weak bullish: the oil and terms-of-trade support now deserves more weight as crude strength helped CAD hold up relatively better even in a defensive market mood. The key limitation remains that CAD is also a risk-sensitive currency with 70%+ of exports to the US, so the crude link can be diluted when the market is in hard risk-off mode. Ivey PMI on Tuesday and Jobs Report on Friday are the key events.
drivers: WTI above $100/bbl — petro-proxy support; Kuwaiti crude carrier struck in Dubai port
January GDP +0.1% (beat); February GDP estimated +0.2% — limited but ongoing momentum
BoC repriced to ~44bps of tightening by year-end on imported inflation fears
Canada-China economic dialogue agreed for H2 2026 — trade diversification positive
Ivey PMI (Tue 15:00 FFT) and Jobs Report (Fri 13:30 FFT) are the key events

currencies.name: Swiss Franc
currencies.rationale: CHF had a surprisingly weak week — failing to capitalize on its traditional safe-haven status despite severe geopolitical escalation. The market clearly preferred USD as the cleaner defensive currency, given its yield advantage. Swiss CPI printed softer than expected (0.3% YoY vs 0.6% exp), though energy-driven inflation pulled it away from the absolute bottom of the SNB's 0-2% target range. Massive month-end/quarter-end rebalancing flows (BofA, Barclays) heavily favored USD buying against CHF. Near-term weak bearish: SNB intervention cap intact, USD yield advantage persists, and the market is not treating CHF as the preferred haven. CHF Unemployment Rate on Wednesday and SNB Chairman Schlegel speaking on Thursday are the key events.
drivers: USD preferred as haven over CHF — yield advantage and intervention risk cap CHF gains
Swiss CPI 0.3% YoY (vs 0.6% exp) — softer than expected; energy uptick provides SNB relief
Massive month-end/quarter-end rebalancing flows favored USD/CHF buying
SNB markets price ~29bps of tightening — global inflation spillover, not domestic pressure
Unemployment Rate (Wed 08:00 FFT) and SNB Schlegel Speaks (Thu 09:00 FFT) are key events

currencies.name: New Zealand Dollar
currencies.rationale: NZD endured another punishing week as one of the primary casualties of the deteriorating global risk environment. The Kiwi showed specific vulnerability — underperforming right out of the gate on Monday compared with G10 peers, a trend that persisted as global stagflation fears mounted. RBNZ has been repriced to ~60bps of tightening by year-end, but this is a response to imported inflation pressure rather than domestic strength. Near-term bearish: NZD is a pro-cyclical, high-beta currency that depreciates in risk-off conditions, with strong positive relationships to broader risk assets and the Australia-China channel. The RBNZ rate decision on Wednesday (hold expected at 2.25%) is the key event — a dovish statement tone would add further pressure.
drivers: High-beta risk proxy — underperformed G10 peers from Monday open
RBNZ repriced to ~60bps of tightening — imported inflation response, not domestic strength
NZD tightly linked to AUD and China channel — both under pressure
Least traded of major currencies — more exposed when positioning moves against cyclicals
RBNZ Rate Decision (Wed 03:00 FFT) — hold expected; statement tone is the key catalyst

long.rationale: Weak Bullish CAD (oil terms-of-trade support, Ivey PMI, Jobs Fri) vs Weak Bearish CHF (SNB intervention cap, USD preferred haven, Schlegel speaks Thu)
Weak Bullish JPY (BOJ hawkish, verbal intervention at 160, Tankan beat) vs Bearish AUD (high-beta risk-off, no domestic catalyst, bank holiday Mon)
Weak Bullish JPY (BOJ normalization, creditor nation, intervention risk) vs Bearish NZD (RBNZ hold Wed, high-beta, pro-cyclical weakness)

short.rationale: Strong Bullish USD (stagflation + CPI Fri + Core PCE Thu) vs Bearish AUD (high-beta risk-off, no data events, bank holiday Mon)
Strong Bullish USD (CPI Fri Critical, FOMC Minutes Wed) vs Bearish NZD (RBNZ hold Wed, high-beta, pro-cyclical)
Weak Bullish CAD (oil support, Ivey PMI Tue, Jobs Fri) vs Bearish AUD (risk-off proxy, no domestic catalyst)
